name: ClaudeForge
description: Automated CLAUDE.md file creation, enhancement, and maintenance for Claude Code projects. Includes a claudeforge-skill, /enhance-claude-md slash command, and a Guardian background agent. Uses SessionStart lifecycle hooks for auto-update checks. Version 2.0 supports modern permissions syntax and hot-reload.

findings_summary.notes: Well-organized Claude Code plugin for CLAUDE.md management. MIT license. Directory structure: skill/, command/, agent/, hooks/, docs/, examples/, test/. The hooks directory contains a single pre-commit.sh (not a Claude Code hook, but a git pre-commit hook). The install.sh performs a standard clone-and-copy installation to ~/.claude/. It supports Windows via install.ps1 as well. Has a CLAUDE.md at root for project context, CHANGELOG.md, and GitHub Actions CI/CD (.github/ directory). The install.sh checks for the presence of skill/, command/, agent/ directories before downloading from GitHub — safe pattern. Guardian agent uses fork_safe: true for non-blocking background operation. No credential access or exfiltration patterns found. Concern: install.sh can run in REMOTE_INSTALL mode (downloading from GitHub) which is the curl-pipe-sh pattern, but implementation is transparent.
